education.degree: Master of Engineering in Computer Science
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

experience.title: Technology Intern
experience.description: As a Technology Intern for the Republican National Committee in summer 2025, I built an internal full-stack web application using Django, Python, and PostgreSQL. I worked across the stack—designing user interfaces, developing RESTful APIs, and improving database performance through query optimization. Collaborating with cross-functional teams, I contributed to delivering a scalable, production-ready tool while strengthening my experience in backend architecture and full-stack development. This internship provided valuable insights into the technology needs of different types of organizations and enhanced my skills in building impactful applications under tight deadlines.

experience.title: Undergraduate Research Assistant
experience.description: During my senior year at Virginia Tech, I worked as an undergraduate research assistant in a Human-Computer Interaction lab, developing a mobile application for Appalachian Trail hikers. I focused on frontend development using React Native and conducted research on effective notification systems to improve engagement in low-connectivity environments. I also worked with and led a team of undergraduate students, contributing to both the technical implementation and overall product design. This experience enhanced my skills in mobile development, user experience design, and team collaboration.

experience.title: Undergraduate Teaching Assistant
experience.organization: Virginia Tech Computer Science Department - CS 3114
experience.description: During the summer of 2024, I worked as an undergraduate teaching assistant for CS 3114 (Data Structures and Algorithms) at Virginia Tech. I guided students through complex topics including object-oriented programming, data structures, and algorithms by leading office hours and providing support through an online forum. Through this role, I improved my ability to break down technical concepts and strengthened my own understanding of foundational computer science topics. This experience also enhanced my communication skills and ability to work with diverse groups of students, fostering a collaborative learning environment.

projects.title: Retail Inventory System (Graduate Capstone Project)
projects.description: Developing a full-stack web application for Marine Corps Community Services to identify inventory issues and provide real-time visibility. Integrated with a machine learning pipeline for automated detection and inventory management. Architected scalable backend services with Spring Boot and developed a responsive frontend for seamless user interaction.

projects.title: Full Stack Budgeting Application
projects.description: Designed and developed a comprehensive budgeting web application that enables users to track income and expenses efficiently. Built a secure backend using FastAPI and SQLModel with JWT-based authentication for data protection. Implemented a dynamic, component-driven frontend using React and TypeScript with intuitive user interface for financial management.

projects.title: Full Stack Bookstore Application
projects.description: Developed a full-stack web application for a bookstore system using a relational database for data persistence. Implemented Java-based backend services with RESTful APIs for seamless data communication. Created a TypeScript/React frontend with reusable components and modern design patterns to provide an engaging user experience.

projects.title: Appalachian Trail Hiker Mobile Application
projects.description: Developed a mobile application designed to enhance the hiking experience on the Appalachian Trail. The application includes features for trail navigation, notification systems for alerts and updates, and user-friendly interface design. Worked as part of a team managing multiple undergraduate student developers.
